original: IBM chess-playing computer Deep Blue becomes the first computer to win a game of chess against a reigning human chess champion,Garry Kasparov
First "Tom and Jerry" cartoon "Puss Gets the Boot" is created byWilliam HannaandJoseph Barberaand released in theaters by MGM
South African PresidentF. W. de KlerkannouncesNelson Mandelawill be freed on February 11th
British battleship HMS Dreadnought launches after only 100 days and renders all other capital ships obsolete with its revolutionary design
Sun Cayun pole vaults female indoor world record (4.12m)
American composer Leroy Anderson completes his orchestral work  'Sleigh Ride' which becomes a worldwide holiday favorite
Biggest oil well gusher ever -Edward L. Doheny's Cerro Azul No. 4 first gushes 600 feet in the air near Tampico, Mexico
Baseball outlaws all pitches involving tampering with ball
Speed skaterBonnie Blairwins 1992 Olympics 1st gold medal for USA
American 2-time world championPeggy Flemingcomfortably wins Olympic women's figure skating gold medal at the Grenoble Winter Games
Bill White becomes 1st African American baseball announcer (NY Yankees)
Bucky Harrisat 27, becomes youngest major league baseball manager (Washington Senators)
26.4 cm precipitation falls on Mount Washington NH (state record)
Lithuania votes for independence from USSR
Glenn Millerand his Orchestra are awarded the first-ever gold record for selling 1 million copies of "Chattanooga Choo Choo"
Judy Ikenberry wins 1st Us women's marathon (2:55:17)
Jani Sievinen swims world record 200m backstroke (1:55.59)
Niagara Falls hydroelectric project begins producing power
Soyuz TM-25 launches to the MIR
American chemistJames Franklin Hydeis granted a patent for fused silica - a glass production breakthrough
John Franklin Enders, American microbiologist (1954 Nobel in Physiology or Medicine for culturing poliovirus, developed measles vaccine), born in West Hartford, Connecticut (d. 1985)
-54°F (-48°C), Seneca, Oregon (state record)
-39°F (-39°C), Milligan, Ohio (state lowest record temperature)
1st US fire extinguisher patent granted to Alanson Crane of Virginia
Andrew Becker demonstrates diving suit
The Postal Telegraph-Cable Company of New York City delivers the 1st singing telegram
"John and Marsha" by Stan Freberg, a parody of daytime soap operas which had only two voices repeating each other's names, peaks at #21 in the USA
Treaty of Paris ends the French-Indian War, France agrees to surrender Canada to Great Britain
Beginning of Mormon march to western US
Comet Shoemaker-Holt 2 Closest Approach to Earth (1.9245 AU)
WW II peace treaties signed
The US Navy evacuate 1000s from Tachen Islands in the Pacific
Johanna Westerdijk installed as the Netherlands' 1st female professor
Jonathan Netanyahu Lane in the Bronx, New York, named in honor of the Bronx-born Israeli soldier who died freeing hostages in Entebbe Raid in Uganda in 1976
Tony Robinson of Jamaica becomes Nottingham's 1st black sheriff
(William) "Judy" Johnson selected to baseball Hall of Fame

Warner Bros. Records releases the eponymous debut album of American rock bandVan Halen; featuring the singles "You Really Got Me", "Runnin' with the Devil", and "Jamie's Cryin'", it becomes a commercial smash with sales of over ten million copies
Test Cricket debut of Aaqib Javed, Pak v NZ age 16 years 189 days
Ras al Khaima joins United Arab Emirates
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51-E mission
Lusia Harris, American Basketball HOF center (AIAW C'ship & MVP 1975–77, Delta State University; Olympic silver 1976), born in Minter City, Mississippi (d. 2022)
(William) "Chick" Webb, American jazz drummer and big band bandleader (introduced Ella Fitzgerald), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1939)
Jerry Goldsmith, American pianist, television and film composer ("The Twilight Zone"; "Planet Of The Apes"), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2004)
James E. West, American engineer and inventor of the electret microphone, born in Prince Edward County, Virginia[1]
Don Wilson, American rock guitarist (The Ventures - "Hawaii 5-0 Theme"; "Walk, Don't Run"), born in Tacoma, Washington (d. 2022)
U-666/U-545/U-283 sink off Ireland
Hobbs & Rhodes make 323 cricket opening stand v Aust at MCG
"Rum and Coca-Cola" by the Andrews Sisters hits #1
"My Friend Flicka" premieres on CBS (later NBC) TV
Dutch Princess Wilhelmina publishes "Lonely, but not alone"
Anti-Nazi "Het Parool" begins publishing in Netherlands
Henry Morton Stanleydeparts for the Congo
Joseph Haydn's 99th Symphony in E premieres, in London, England
Edmond Halleyis appointed as the second Astronomer Royal at the Greenwich Observatory
Canadian world heavyweight boxing championTommy BurnsKOs Englishman Jack Palmer in round 4 in London in his 8th title defense
St. Paul is thought to have been shipwrecked at Malta
U Cit Tok', pretender to the throne of the Maya city of Copán assumes control of the city (as inscribed on Altar L)
Jozef Haller de Hallenburg performs symbolic wedding of Poland to the sea, celebrating restitution of Polish access to open sea
Walraven van Hall, Dutch banker who led resistance against Nazis, born in Amsterdam, Netherlands (d. 1945)
Dominique Pire, Belgian Dominican friar (1958 Nobel Peace Prize for aiding WW II refugees), born in Dinant, Belgium (d. 1969)
